sovereign_credit_rating.value: S&P: AA+, Moody's: Aa1, Fitch: AA+

resource_self_sufficiency_index.methodology: Average of min(production/consumption, 1.0) for energy, food, water, minerals

political_risk_premium_bps.methodology: Sovereign rating implied spread + adjustments for instability, conflict, sanctions, autocracy

market_accessibility_score.methodology: Average of capital_account_openness, stock_market_cap/200, financial_dev_index, ease_of_business/100

narrative.executive_summary: The US faces converging crises this week: the SCOTUS struck down IEEPA tariffs 6-3, prompting Trump to impose 15% global tariffs under Section 122. Additional 10% China tariffs (20% total) and 25% Canada/Mexico tariffs take effect March 4. US-Israeli Operation Epic Fury struck 1,000+ targets in Iran -- the largest US military operation since Iraq 2003 -- with 3 US soldiers killed in Iranian retaliation. Recession probability jumped from 25-30% to 40-50%. Dow futures dropped 517 points. Defense spending at $997B. Fed holds at 3.50-3.75%.
key_changes_this_week: SCOTUS struck down IEEPA tariffs 6-3; Trump pivoted to Section 122 with 15% global tariff (transformative)
Additional 10% China tariff (20% total) and 25% Canada/Mexico tariffs effective March 4 (major)
US-Israeli strikes hit 1,000+ Iran targets; 3 US soldiers killed in Kuwait (transformative)
Recession probability surged from 25-30% to 40-50%; Dow futures -517 points
S&P 500 posted largest monthly decline in a year; safe-haven rotation to USD, gold, Treasuries
Fed holds at 3.50-3.75%; oil shock creates stagflation risk limiting future rate cuts
narrative.outlook: The US faces a potential stagflation scenario: oil price shock from Hormuz disruption pushes inflation higher while recession probability rises on trade disruption and consumer confidence deterioration. The constitutional crisis over trade policy creates legal uncertainty for hundreds of billions in tariff collections. March FOMC (17-18) is a key decision point. Trump-Xi summit March 30 could either escalate or de-escalate China trade tensions.
narrative.investor_implications: Defensive positioning warranted: overweight Treasuries (10Y below 4%), gold ($5,278), energy, and defense. Underweight consumer discretionary, airlines, and trade-exposed manufacturers. Monitor VIX spike on Monday open. The $200B tariff refund question creates contingent liability for US fiscal position. Defense contractors (Lockheed, Raytheon, Northrop) are direct beneficiaries of Iran operations.
narrative.data_quality_note: US macro data is high quality. Recession probability estimates are analyst consensus, not official forecasts. Market futures data is pre-Monday-open and may shift significantly.
